_comment: Speedrun LSAT AI generator knowledge base. Every generatable flaw is grounded in a free, credible, human-written source (cited by URL). The generator instantiates NEW LR 'Flaw' stimuli from these definitions; it never copies source text verbatim.
stem: The reasoning above is most vulnerable to criticism on the grounds that it

flaws.name: affirming the consequent
flaws.answer: concludes that a sufficient condition was met merely because the result it guarantees occurred

flaws.name: denying the antecedent
flaws.answer: concludes that a result will not occur merely because one sufficient condition for it is absent

flaws.name: correlation treated as causation
flaws.answer: infers a causal relationship from the mere fact that two things occur together

flaws.name: hasty generalization
flaws.answer: draws a general conclusion from a sample that is too small or unrepresentative to support it

flaws.name: circular reasoning
flaws.answer: presupposes the truth of the very conclusion it is trying to establish

flaws.name: ad hominem
flaws.answer: rejects a claim by criticizing the person who makes it rather than the claim itself

flaws.name: false dilemma
flaws.answer: treats two options as the only possibilities when other alternatives are available

flaws.name: equivocation
flaws.answer: relies on a key term being used in two different senses

flaws.name: improper appeal to authority
flaws.answer: takes an authority's opinion as decisive on a matter outside that authority's expertise

flaws.name: fallacy of composition
flaws.answer: assumes that what is true of each part must be true of the whole

conditional.p_neg: This bill did not have majority support
Dana did not master the material
conditional.q_neg: she did not pass the exam

conditional.p_neg: This rod was not heated sufficiently

conditional.q_neg: it is not busy on weekends

causal: Counties that expanded public transit last year reported lower average commute times. Clearly, expanding public transit is what shortened commutes in those counties.
Students who eat breakfast tend to score higher on tests. Therefore, eating breakfast must be causing the higher scores.
As ice cream sales rose over the summer, so did the number of sunburns. So buying ice cream evidently leads people to get sunburned.
Employees who adopted the new scheduling app finished projects faster. It follows that the app is responsible for their speed.
sample: A reporter interviewed eight people leaving a luxury car dealership and found that most planned to vote for the incumbent. The reporter concluded that most voters in the state support the incumbent.
In a poll of fifteen readers of a cycling magazine, nearly all favored building more bike lanes. The magazine concluded that the city's residents favor more bike lanes.
A critic tried two dishes at a new restaurant, disliked both, and wrote that everything on the menu is poorly prepared.
A manager saw that the three employees who complained most were all on the sales team and concluded that the sales team is the company's least satisfied department.
